no_changes_rationale:
Bead ddx-05b4cc9d: no new changes needed — all AC already satisfied by prior
execute-bead attempts merged to main and present at the base rev of this
worktree (a21580e1).

## What is done

AC1 — Root-cause fix (Option 1, id→path at filter site)
    cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go lines 267-299, landed in commit
    818b5c9e "fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". GetWorkersGraphQL now calls s.GetProjectByID(projectID),
    derives expectedPath = proj.Path, and compares rec.ProjectRoot to the
    resolved path. Unknown project id returns empty (not error).

AC2 — Two-project regression test
    cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go —
    TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new) seeds two registered projects
    with distinct paths/ids, writes a worker targeting each, and asserts:
      * workersByProject(id=A) returns only worker A
      * workersByProject(id=B) returns only worker B
      * workers (global) returns both
      * workersByProject(id=unknown) returns empty (no GraphQL error)
    TestGraphQLWorkers was also updated to query by resolved project id,
    so it actually exercises the fix.

    Verified locally:
      $ cd cli && go test -run "TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID|TestGraphQLWorkers" ./internal/server/ -v
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkers (0.01s)
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (0.01s)
      ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server

AC3 — Backward-compat test for pre-migration worker records
    N/A. AC3 is conditional on choosing Option 2 (add ProjectID field to
    WorkerRecord). Option 1 was chosen; WorkerRecord schema is unchanged
    and no migration path exists, so the compatibility concern does not
    apply.

AC4 — Playwright e2e validation
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts lines 129-226, landed
    in commit 5f234aad "test(ui): cover project-scoped drain worker flow
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". The test navigates to the project home page, clicks
    Drain queue, confirms the dialog, waits, navigates to the Workers tab,
    and asserts exactly one row with state "running" and the matching
    worker id prefix. Then it navigates to a second project's Workers tab
    and asserts that project-1's worker does not appear and the empty-state
    message is visible.

AC5 — No regression to unrelated surfaces
    * Global workers query still returns all workers — covered by the
      "global workers returns both" assertion in AC2's regression test.
    * Worker detail page loads by worker id — GetWorkerGraphQL(id) was
      not touched by the fix; it keys on worker id, not project id/path.
    * workerProgress subscription patches rows in place — the subscription
      resolver was not modified; only the list-filter comparison changed.

AC6 — Cross-reference to ddx-69789664
    Present in the 818b5c9e commit message:
      "Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
       acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly."

## Why nothing changes in this attempt

The bead's <notes> section contains BLOCKING review findings from the first
attempt (before 818b5c9e landed). Attempts 1 and 2 already addressed each
finding:

  818b5c9e — server fix + first regression test (AC1, AC2, AC6)
  5f234aad — Playwright e2e (AC4)

Both commits are ancestors of HEAD (a21580e1):
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 818b5c9e HEAD  # true
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 5f234aad HEAD  # true

The files in the worktree already contain the fix and the tests. Running
the tests shows they pass. Re-committing the same changes would produce
an empty diff or duplicate history. A well-justified no-changes is the
correct outcome here; the bead should be closed by the reviewer on the
basis of the evidence already in main.

## What a reviewer should check to confirm

  git log --oneline -- cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go \
      cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go \
      cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts
  # expect to see 818b5c9e and 5f234aad

  grep -n expectedPath cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
  # expect the id→path resolution in GetWorkersGraphQL

  cd cli && go test -run TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID ./internal/server/ -v
  # expect PASS
